title: Dental nurses
description: Administer oral hygiene care to patients. Assess patient oral hygiene problems or needs and maintain health records. Advise patients on oral health maintenance and disease prevention. May provide advanced care such as providing fluoride treatment or administering topical anesthesia. Transport patients to areas such as operating rooms or x-ray rooms using wheelchairs, stretchers, or moveable beds. May maintain stocks of supplies or clean and transport equipment. Psychiatric orderlies are included in Psychiatric Aides. Under close supervision of an occupational therapist or occupational therapy assistant, perform only delegated, selected, or routine tasks in specific situations. These duties include preparing patient and treatment room. (Based on 11 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Clean calcareous deposits, accretions, and stains from teeth and beneath margins of gums, using dental instruments.
Administer local anesthetic agents and apply protective coating of fluoride to teeth.
Maintain dental equipment, sharpen and sterilize dental instruments, and disinfect equipment using germicides or sterilizing equipment.
Provide clinical services or health education to improve and maintain the oral health of patients or the general public and instruct patients in oral hygiene and plaque control programs.
Record and review patient medical histories, including taking and recording medical and dental histories and vital signs of patients.
Feel and visually examine gums for sores and signs of disease, using probes, to locate periodontal recessed gums and signs of gum disease.
Chart conditions of decay and disease for diagnosis and treatment by dentist, and document treatment information in patient records.
Expose and develop x-ray film, including exposing dental diagnostic x-rays and transporting specimens or fluid samples to laboratories.
Conduct dental health clinics for community groups to augment services of dentist and attend continuing education courses to maintain or update skills.
Manage stocks of dental supplies and equipment inventory, and order and monitor supplies as needed.
Transport patients to treatment areas, such as operating rooms, using wheelchairs, stretchers, or moveable beds.
Assist dentist in management of medical or dental emergencies and provide postoperative instructions prescribed by dentist.
Make preliminary impressions for study casts and pour, trim, and polish study casts.
Prepare and maintain work area, materials, and equipment, setting up instrument trays and preparing materials or assisting dentist during dental procedures.
Clean and organize work area and disinfect equipment after treatment, including cleaning instruments to prepare them for sterilization.
Conduct in-service training sessions or family and community education programs, and assist in educational activities relevant to dental health.
Observe patients during treatment to compile and evaluate data on patients' responses and progress, reporting findings to dentist.
Collect and transport hazardous waste in accordance with applicable regulations when cleaning and sanitizing patient areas.
Collect and compile data to document patient performance or assess program quality.
Assist in the remediation or development of patients' speech and language skills as directed by speech-language pathologists.
Assist in conducting patient assessments of language, voice, fluency, articulation, or hearing.
Place devices, such as blood pressure cuffs and pulse oximeters, on patients to monitor vital signs as needed.
Read literature or participate in professional organizations to keep updated on best practices.
Conduct training sessions to disseminate information regarding equipment or instruments used in procedures.
Collect specimens from patients using standard medical procedures when required.
Attend training sessions to refresh basic professional skills.
Assist in the conduct of medical procedures as directed by physicians or registered nurses.
Position or transport patients according to instructions from medical personnel.
Maintain inventories of medical equipment and supplies to ensure availability for procedures.
